method_atom: A framework for a spatially-homogeneous but directionally-anisotropic dynamical system driven by a source whose effective pressure deviates from isotropy, parametrized by a small number of skewness parameters that quantify the departure from isotropic response along different axes. The transferable machinery couples the evolution of the anisotropic background to a covariant treatment of linear perturbations sourced by an imperfect (anisotropic) stress, and constrains the anisotropy parameters against directional/angular observations, where the leading anisotropic signature appears as a low-order multipole (quadrupole) contribution.
object_atom: The accelerated expansion of the Universe driven by dark energy with an anisotropic equation of state, modeled in a Bianchi I cosmology and confronted with the redshift and angular distribution of type Ia supernovae and with cosmic microwave background anisotropies, with implications for galaxy formation and connections to Lorentz-violating fundamental theories.
brief_reason: The anisotropic-source-plus-skewness-parameter dynamics and covariant perturbation analysis constrained by angular/multipole data carry across fields, while the accelerating-Universe / dark-energy / Bianchi-I-cosmology subject is the cosmology-specific object.
